title: Why MCP and ChatGPT Apps Use Double Iframes — Frédéric Barthelet, Alpic
summary: ChatGPT apps and MCP use double iframe architecture to isolate third-party code while preserving functionality. The outer iframe loads from a controlled subdomain with a lightweight script, which then loads the actual app HTML via srcdoc into an inner frame, preventing CSP breaches and sandbox escapes while blocking cross-app storage collisions.
writeup: This talk explains the double iframe architecture behind ChatGPT apps and MCP, which exists to isolate third-party code while keeping app functionality intact. The outer iframe loads from a controlled subdomain running a lightweight script, which then injects the real app HTML into an inner frame via srcdoc. The structure matters because it prevents CSP breaches and sandbox escapes and blocks cross-app storage collisions, tying together same-origin policy, sandbox, and srcdoc behavior.
